tool_call.name: retrieve_information
tool_call.args: {"prompt": "What is the AI capex or AI-related capital expenditure for Q1 2026 and any other periods? Retrieve all disclosures about AI capital expenditures, AI infrastructure spending, and related capex figures from {{spacex_s1}}."}

parsed_args.prompt: What is the AI capex or AI-related capital expenditure for Q1 2026 and any other periods? Retrieve all disclosures about AI capital expenditures, AI infrastructure spending, and related capex figures from {{spacex_s1}}.

tool_output.output:
Based on the provided excerpts from the company’s S-1 filing, here are all disclosed AI-related capital expenditures (capex), AI infrastructure spending figures, and related commitments.

---

### 1. AI Capital Expenditures by Period (from Segment Reporting)

The company reports capex in three segments: Space, Connectivity, and AI. The AI capex figures are as follows:

| Period | AI Capital Expenditures |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Q1 2026 (three months ended March 31, 2026)** | **$7,723 million** |
| Q1 2025 (three months ended March 31, 2025) | $2,567 million |
| FY 2025 (year ended December 31, 2025) | $12,727 million |
| FY 2024 (year ended December 31, 2024) | $5,633 million |
| FY 2023 (year ended December 31, 2023) | $463 million |

**Source snippet (appears in multiple sections):**
> *“The following table presents our capital expenditures by segment:*
> *|  | Three Months Ended March 31, |  | Year Ended December 31, |
> |  | 2026 |  | 2025 |  | 2025 |  | 2024 |  | 2023 |
> | AI ........................................................ | 7,723 |  | 2,567 |  | 12,727 |  | 5,633 |  | 463 |
> | Total Capital Expenditures ................. | $10,107 |  | $4,140 |  | $20,737 |  | $11,163 |  | $4,415 |”*

**Also directly stated in narrative:**
> *“• For the three months ended March 31, 2026, capital expenditures for our Space segment was $1,052 million, for our Connectivity segment was $1,332 million and for our AI segment was $7,723 million. In 2025, capital expenditures for our Space segment was $3,832 million, for our Connectivity segment was $4,178 million and for our AI segment was $12,727 million.”*

---

### 2. Year-over-Year Changes and Explanations for AI Capex

**Q1 2026 vs. Q1 2025**
> *“AI capital expenditures for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased $5,156 million to $7,723 million compared to $2,567 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025. The increase was primarily driven by investments in the rapid expansion of our terrestrial data centers, including the development, construction, and equipping of new facilities and supporting infrastructure.”*

**FY 2025 vs. FY 2024 vs. FY 2023**
> *“AI capital expenditures for 2025 increased $7,094 million to $12,727 million compared to $5,633 million in 2024, while AI capital expenditures for 2024 increased $5,170 million to $5,633 million compared to $463 million in 2023. This increase was primarily driven by significant investments in the rapid expansion of our terrestrial data centers, including the development, construction, and equipping of new facilities and supporting infrastructure.”*

---

### 3. AI Infrastructure within Property, Plant, and Equipment (Balance Sheet)

As of March 31, 2026 and December 31, 2025, the company’s PP&E includes assets directly tied to AI infrastructure:

| (in millions) | March 31, 2026 | December 31, 2025 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Servers and networking equipment | $23,850 | $22,694 |
| Data center infrastructure | 2,965 | 2,960 |
| Construction-in-progress | 14,045 | 4,604 |

**Context on Construction-in-Progress:**
> *“Construction in progress is primarily comprised of ongoing construction and expansion of the facilities and equipment as well as AI infrastructure that has not yet been placed in service.”*

The jump in Construction-in-progress from $4,604 million to $14,045 million during Q1 2026 largely reflects the rapid build-out of AI data centers.

---

### 4. Capitalized Interest Related to AI Infrastructure

For Q1 2026, the company capitalized interest on AI infrastructure projects:

> *“Interest is capitalized during the construction period for significant long term construction projects, such as the AI infrastructure data centers and launch facilities. For the three months ended March 31, 2026, the Company capitalized $7 million of interest, which is included in Construction-in-progress amounts above. No interest was capitalized during the three months ended March 31, 2025.”*

---

### 5. AI-Related Contractual Commitments (Future Spending)

The company’s non-cancelable contractual commitments are primarily for AI infrastructure and cloud capacity. A maturity schedule is provided (amounts presumably as of March 31, 2026):

| Year | Commitment |
| :--- | :--- |
| 2026 | $2,720 million |
| 2027 | $21,476 million |
| 2028 | $1,250 million |
| 2029 | $4 million |
| 2030 | $1 million |
| **Total** | **$25,451 million** |

> *“The Company’s unconditional obligations are non-cancelable contractual commitments primarily relate to the Company’s investments in AI infrastructure and third-party cloud capacity arrangements and other service arrangements.”*

Additionally, during Q1 2026, a new purchase agreement was signed specifically for AI infrastructure turbines:

> *“During the three months ended March 31, 2026, there have been no material changes to the Company’s unconditional obligation since December 31, 2025 other than the execution of certain purchase agreements with an unaffiliated third party to acquire additional turbines for the AI infrastructure totaling $925 million through 2029.”*

---

### 6. Strategic Context and Forward-Looking AI Spend

The MD&A outlines the company’s intention to continue investing heavily in AI compute infrastructure:

> *“We acquired xAI in February 2026, which forms the basis of our AI segment. We expect to allocate substantial capital to expand our compute infrastructure, and we expect a multi-year investment horizon before these deployments translate into sustained positive AI Segment Adjusted EBITDA. During this investment period, our capital expenditures will scale as quickly as we are able to deploy power and compute to address the $26.5 trillion potential market opportunity for AI.”*

A supplemental segment disclosure for Q1 2026 also shows the AI segment’s depreciation and amortization:
> *“Depreciation and amortization ............................ | $166 |  | $783 |  | $1,493 |  | $2,442 |”*  
(The $1,493 million is for the AI segment.)

---

### 7. Risk Factors Highlighting AI Capex Requirements

Several risk factors underscore the scale and importance of AI capital spending:

> *“The development and maintenance of the technologies and infrastructure necessary to support our current and future operations will require significant capital expenditures, and if we are unable to generate sufficient cash flow from operations or obtain additional financing on acceptable terms, our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects could be materially and adversely affected.”*

> *“• the amount, nature and timing of our capital expenditures and the impact of such capital expenditures on our growth and performance, including our ability to fund such expenditures, manage costs, strategically reduce costs and achieve expected returns on investment;”*

> *“• our ability to obtain sufficient power, GPUs, and other critical components and manage our supply chain to meet our needs;”*

All of the above disclosures directly relate to AI capital expenditures and AI infrastructure spending as derived from the provided text.

| **Q1 2026** | $1,052 | $1,332 | $7,723 | $10,107 |
